$comment: Agentic QE Framework v2 — User-configurable settings. Agents read this file instead of using hardcoded values.

exploration.$comment: maxAttemptsPerStep: how many times Explorer retries a failing interaction before flagging in enriched.md

executor.$comment: maxCycles: how many fix-and-rerun cycles before stopping. preFlightEnabled: run tsc + fidelity check

timeouts.$comment: testTimeoutMs: per-test timeout (increase for long E2E scenarios). Override these for slower enterprise apps. Values in milliseconds.

enrichedScenario.$comment: filenameSuffix: appended to scenario name for enriched version. createOnExploration: Explorer writes enriched file. neverModifyExisting: once created, Explorer never edits.

bugDetection.$comment: Controls bug detection sensitivity. uncertainVerdictEnabled: allow UNCERTAIN verdict for ambiguous cases.

builder.$comment: incrementalUpdate: when true, Builder runs scenario-diff.js and only updates changed steps. preserveHealedCode: never overwrite code with // HEALED comments. preservePacingComments: never remove // PACING: waits.

chunking.$comment: maxStepsPerChunk: maximum steps per Explorer chunk (if Explorer verification is chunked for long scenarios). alwaysChunk: false for Copilot (subagents share 200K context window — chunking adds risk with no benefit). Set true only for Claude Code CLI where subagents get independent context windows.

appContext.$comment: Per-type app-context filenames in scenarios/app-contexts/. The recommended model is ONE REPO PER APPLICATION — a team owns one app that may have web + mobile + API sides, and all three sit in this block. Each team fills in their own filenames for the application they automate (e.g., 'web': 'my-app.md'). Agents MUST resolve the filename from this block by the scenario's type (and platform for mobile), not from the scenario name, folder, or URL. Resolution rules: web → appContext.web; api → appContext.api; hybrid → appContext.web (+ optionally merge appContext.api); mobile → appContext.mobile.{android|ios} based on PLATFORM env var at runtime; mobile-hybrid → appContext.mobile.{android|ios} (+ optionally merge appContext.api). Empty string means 'no app-context for this type — skip the load cleanly.' For mobile, android and ios split because UI frameworks (Jetpack Compose vs SwiftUI), permission flows, and gesture patterns often differ enough to warrant separate learned-pattern files; use the same filename in both slots if your app shares enough across platforms. `hybrid` and `mobile-hybrid` are NOT separate keys here because they are scenario compositions (UI + API calls) that reuse the underlying type's app-context. The API slot is often empty — useful only when the API has non-standard auth, pagination, or error-code idioms that need documentation. The legacy flat `filename` key from earlier releases is NO LONGER READ — migrate by moving the filename under the slot matching your primary scenario type.

mobile.$comment: Mobile-specific settings. defaultPlatform used when scenario says Platform: both. Mobile timeouts are higher because device interactions are slower than browser.

observability.$comment: All agents MUST populate observability fields in their metrics JSON. metricsVersion tracks schema changes. If platform does not expose token counts, agents write 'Platform does not expose token count' instead of omitting the field.
